question: Did the review adhere to pre-defined objectives and eligibility criteria?
Were the eligibility crieria appropriate for the review question?
answers:
INTRO - we reviewed neurological adverse events (AEs) reported with TNF-a inhibitors to the Food and Drug Administration Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS) utilising a standardised scoring tool to estimate causality in purported drug-induced AEs.

A search of FAERS for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

question: Were eligibility criteria unambiguous?
answers: A search of FAERS (Food and Drug Administration Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS)) for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

question: Were all restrictions in eligibility crieria based on study characteristics appropriate (e.g. date, sample size, study quality, outcomes measured)? If yes, indicate which study characteristic was an inclusion/exclusion criteria
answers:
Individual files were downloaded for the period January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009 and analysed

The database was searched for neurological AEs following treatment with the following TNF-a inhibitors: infliximab (first approved for CD in 1998), etanercept (first approved for RA in 1998), adalimumab (first approved for RA in 2002) and certolizumab (first approved for CD in 2008) and their trade names. Golimumab was not included in the search as it was only approved in October 2009 and the duration since approval was considered too short to develop neurological AEs related to its use.

question: Were any restrictions in eligibility criteria based on sources of info appropriate (e.g. publication status or format, language, availability of data)?
Did the search include an appropriate range of databases/ electronic sources for published and unpublished reports?
answers: A search of FAERS for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

question: Were methods additional to database searching used to identify relevant reports?

answers: The database was searched for neurological AEs following treatment with the following TNF-a inhibitors: infliximab (first approved for CD in 1998), etanercept (first approved for RA in 1998), adalimumab (first approved for RA in 2002) and certolizumab (first approved for CD in 2008) and their trade names.

question: Were search strategy restrictions based on date, publication format, or language appropriate?
answers: we reviewed neurological adverse events (AEs) reported with TNF-a inhibitors to the Food and Drug Administration Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS).

question: Was between-study variation (heterogeneity) minimal or addressed in the synthesis?
Were the findings robust, e.g. as demonstrated through funnel plot or sensitivity analyses?

Did the interpretation of findings address all of the concerns identified in Domains 1 to 4?
answers: Limitations of the FAERS including both under reporting  as well as disproportionate reporting of certain AEs with particular drugs by lawyers have been described.

A search of FAERS for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

Did the research questions and inclusion criteria for the review include the components of PICO?
answers: A search of FAERS (Food and Drug Administration Adverse Event Reporting System (FAERS)) for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

question: Did the review authors explain their selection of the study designs for inclusion in the review?
answers: Limitations of the FAERS including both under reporting as well as disproportionate reporting of certain AEs with particular drugs by lawyers have been described. Nonetheless, this database has been used effectively to analyse previous AE reports of TNF-a inhibitors.

answers: A search of FAERS for neurological AEs (January 1, 2000 to December 31, 2009) reported with infliximab, adalimumab, certolizumab and etanercept was performed.

question: Did the review authors provide a satisfactory explanation for, and discussion of, any heterogeneity observed in the results of the review?
